med_suggestions.sig: 4 drops in affected ear BID x 7 days

med_suggestions.exclusions: If severe canal edema prevents drops or severe pain out of proportion, requires in-person evaluation.

med_suggestions.notes: ONLY when TM is known intact and no neomycin allergy.
med_suggestions.exclusions: Avoid if tympanic membrane perforation suspected or known; avoid neomycin sensitivity.

note.soap:
S:  
34-year-old adult female with possible pregnancy presents with 5 days of right ear pain. She reports ear discharge, tenderness over the tragus, itching, and recent swimming. She denies fever, upper respiratory infection symptoms, and vertigo. No history of diabetes or immune compromise.

O:  
No physical exam or vital signs available due to phone-only encounter.

A:  
 - Working diagnosis (most likely): Otitis externa (swimmer’s ear)  
 - Differential:  
  1. Otitis externa  
  2. Acute otitis media with perforation  
  3. Foreign body or trauma to ear canal  
  4. Contact dermatitis or allergic reaction of ear canal  
 - Red flags considered: No fever, no vertigo, no systemic symptoms, no signs of severe infection or cellulitis.

P:  
 - Disposition: Manage as outpatient with close follow-up if symptoms worsen or fail to improve.  
 - Plan: Ofloxacin otic drops — 10 drops in affected ear once daily x 7 days (or 5 drops BID x 7 days).  Alternative: Ciprofloxacin/dexamethasone otic drops (4 drops in affected ear BID x 7 days).
   Medication protocol (from data/protocols/meds):
   - Medication recommendations (Tier 1):
   - Reviewing physician will confirm appropriateness and prescribe if indicated.
   - Ofloxacin otic drops — 10 drops in affected ear once daily x 7 days (or 5 drops BID x 7 days)
     - Notes: FIRST-LINE for telephone care when TM status is unknown or otorrhea is present; non-ototoxic and safe with TM perforation.
     - Exclusions: Use otic formulation only.
   - Ciprofloxacin/dexamethasone otic drops — 4 drops in affected ear BID x 7 days
     - Notes: SECOND-LINE; consider when inflammation is prominent or if inadequate response to ofloxacin. Often preferred when TM integrity is known.
     - Exclusions: If severe canal edema prevents drops or severe pain out of proportion, requires in-person evaluation.
   - Neomycin/polymyxin B/hydrocortisone otic drops — 4 drops TID–QID x 7 days
     - Notes: ONLY when TM is known intact and no neomycin allergy.
     - Exclusions: Avoid if tympanic membrane perforation suspected or known; avoid neomycin sensitivity.
   - Supportive care:
   - Keep ear dry: Avoid swimming or water exposure; no cotton swabs; ear protection for showers.
   - Pain control: Acetaminophen or ibuprofen if safe (avoid NSAIDs in late pregnancy, kidney disease, ulcer/GI bleed risk).
   - Follow-up and safety:
   - Re-evaluation: If not improving within 48–72 hours or worsening pain, swelling, or drainage, seek in-person evaluation.
   - Urgent/ED now: Severe pain out of proportion, fever with diabetes/immunocompromise, facial weakness, severe dizziness/vertigo, mastoid swelling/tenderness, altered mental status.
 - Safety net: Advise patient to seek urgent care if she develops fever, worsening pain, spreading redness, swelling, hearing loss, vertigo, or if symptoms persist beyond 7 days despite treatment.
patient_instructions: If you are not improving as expected, or if symptoms worsen, seek in-person medical care.
